target.title: Retry node shell fallback on ENOENT
target.body:
## Summary

- Retry node-host `system.run` shell execution with trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` when the default `/bin/sh` spawn fails with ENOENT.
- Keep PATH-resolved shell fallback out of the retry chain so execution does not switch to an inherited-PATH binary after approval analysis.
- Keep non-shell and Windows invocations unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for the runtime retry chain.

Fixes #85202

## Why

Linux headless node hosts can report `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT` even when the node is connected and `system.which`/manual host checks show a usable shell at `/usr/bin/sh`. The previous node-host path treated the first spawn error as terminal, so `exec host=node` failed before trying another trusted POSIX shell path.

## Verification

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts src/infra/node-shell.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.sanitize-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed; 24 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Node-host `system.run` no longer treats a missing default `/bin/sh` spawn as the only possible POSIX shell path; the patched node host retries trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` on ENOENT.
- **Security-boundary note:** The previous PATH-resolved `sh` fallback was removed after review. The retry chain now uses only absolute shell paths.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the focused test-projects command above from the patched checkout. The regression test imports the actual node-host `runCommand` implementation and simulates `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT`, then successful `/usr/bin/sh` execution.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused command passed 3 Vitest shards / 24 tests, then `pnpm tsgo:core:test` and `git diff --check` completed successfully.
- **Observed result after fix:** The node-host retry path continued after `/bin/sh` ENOENT and returned success from `/usr/bin/sh` with stdout `NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK`.
- **What was not tested:** I do not have the reporter's exact Linux headless node/systemd setup, so I could not reproduce their original `/bin/sh` ENOENT on a connected remote node before patch. The PR keeps the fallback narrow and should still benefit from reporter or maintainer live validation on the exact #85202 environment.
target.author: clawsean
